reviews.review_msg: He's a nice prof overall. The classes got a little confusing towards the end and some of the practice problems aren't solvable. BUT, he does give out a lot of practice questions and worksheets and as long as you understand the core concepts, you should be good.

This course bans calculators, but after Test 1 (linear algebra), you won't need one. He shares more practice problems as the term goes on. He's passionate about econ and open to talk beyond the course. It's not pure math—more like thinking mathematically in an econ way, great for students without strong math background.
Overall, I would recommend Professor Freeman to anyone looking to deepen their understanding of economics in a challenging yet manageable course.

David lives inside his own head. He thinks he is brilliant yet the majority of his slides include structural errors that throw the class on a loop. There are frequent errors in his slides and lectures as he arrives to class unprepared. While I received strong grades in the course, it was due to reading the textbook - his lectures were useless

A super open-minded and super nice professor!! It is not hard to follow his pace in class as long as you concentrate, understanding practice questions in lectures and tutorials are essential. Exams are similar to sample exams. Honestly, he didn't complicate this course. Very patient with students! My favorite professor in SFU!!!

He has a lot of passion for the subject, but he has problems articulating and teaching it to the class. There is at least 1 mistake (likely multiple) every lecture, very long tests within short time frames, and a math-heavy approach that he doesn't explain well. Avoid if you can, but if not, prepare to teach yourself w/ textbook and videos.
Brutal exams. Curve and homework saved me in the end.
I tried so hard and got so far But in the end, it doesn't even matter. It is pretty funny to read all the hate about him, but the real thing that it is 99.9% true

He seems to not prepare enough for the lecture because he needs to correct his powerpoint mistakes every class! It's waste of time for us to wait for him to correct his mistakes and explain his not so clear concepts once again. The essay question is also ridiculous and hard in tests.

Worst teacher ever ... slides are often wrong. Tests are extraordinary hard and he cannot teach the material. He is tough marker too. For his first midterm the highest mark in class for the only short answer question was 4/10. Avoid taking course with him
